mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in higher cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.0%, it signals a labor market inflection point that reduces recession fears and compresses equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales revenue indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which boosts cyclical equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero 20d forward returns in over 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase discount rates on future corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 1.5% signals strong manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances following the INDPRO threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the consumer price index erode real purchasing power and trigger inflation hedging outflows from equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL rises consecutively for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 20d forward returns in over 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust GDP growth exceeding 3% annualized, rising housing starts amplify the demand-driven earnings upgrade cycle, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as capital expenditure accelerates.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where both GDP growth and housing starts are elevated.
